title: Dissection and fixation of murine colonic tissue for myenteric plexus visualization
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"dgc4g","text":"Protocol for harvest of colonic intestinal tissue, with the intent of imaging the myenteric plexus.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"bjf3p","text":"Fixatives can be toxic. Exercise proper use of PPE when handling.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"7pdgm","text":"Zamboni fixative can be purchased commercially or 1.5% picric acid and 2% paraformaldehyde in 0.1N PBS.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"54q1c","text":"Aquamount mounting media is commercially available","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: A ventral midline incision is made and the whole colon is carefully excised into a Sylguard lined dissection dish.
Cut along the mesenteric border until the colonic tube is now a rectanular in shape.
Pin the colon at 110% of length and width mucosa side up. Gently remove the mucosal layer. Re-pin in a new Sylguard lined dish at 100% ensuring tissue is taught but not stretched.
Fix tissue using ice cold Zamboni fixative for 15 minutes.
Remove tissue from dish and wash 3 times in PBS for 15 minutes each wash.
Cut tissue into 3 sections; oral, middle and anal (in order to fit on slide) and mount on glass slide using Aquamount mounting media and a glass cover slip.
creator: Dante Heredia
